title: Slow-cooker lamb shanks
subtitle: Braised lamb shanks with red wine, tomatoes and rosemary.
excerpt: Lamb shanks browned and braised all day in a slow cooker with red wine, tinned tomatoes, garlic and rosemary until the meat comes away from the bone. A dinner worth coming home to. Serves four.
sourceNotes: Braised lamb shanks. Slow-braised lamb shanks in wine and aromatics belong to the tradition of Italian osso buco and French navarin d'agneau, both of which use tough connective-tissue cuts cooked long and slow in liquid. The slow cooker is a domestic adaptation of the same principle: the collagen in the shank dissolves over many hours into gelatin, giving the cooking liquid its characteristic body.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze in the braising liquid. Defrost overnight and reheat gently.
recipe.batchNotes: Make a double batch and freeze half.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cook the day before. The flavour improves overnight in the fridge.
recipe.temperatureNote: Slow cooker on LOW for 7 to 8 hours, or HIGH for 4 to 5 hours.

content.text: The browning step matters. Searing the shanks before they go into the slow cooker builds a depth of flavour that the slow cooker alone cannot produce. Do not crowd the pan when browning: work in batches if necessary and get real colour on all sides.

items.prepNote: lamb or beef, dissolved in 200 ml boiling water

content.text: Season {{lamb-shanks}} of lamb shanks generously with {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt and {{black-pepper}} of pepper. Heat {{rapeseed-oil}} of oil in a wide, heavy frying pan over a high heat until smoking. Brown the shanks all over in batches, 3 to 4 minutes per side, until deeply coloured. Transfer to the slow cooker.
Build the sauce and slow cook

In the same pan, fry {{onion}} of onion, {{carrot}} of carrot and {{celery}} of celery over a medium heat for 5 minutes until softened. Add {{garlic}} of garlic and {{tomato-puree}} of tomato purée and cook for 1 minute. Pour in {{red-wine}} of red wine and scrape up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Tip everything into the slow cooker along with {{tinned-tomatoes}} of tinned tomatoes, the dissolved {{stock-cube}} of stock cube, {{rosemary}} of rosemary sprigs and {{bay-leaves}} of bay leaves. Cook on LOW for 7 to 8 hours, or HIGH for 4 to 5 hours, until the meat is very tender and coming away from the bone cleanly. Remove the rosemary and bay leaves. Taste and adjust seasoning.

items.symptom: Meat still tough after cooking time
items.cause: Slow cooker too full, or not running hot enough on LOW.
items.fix: Lamb shanks need a minimum of 7 hours on LOW. Add another hour and check again. The meat should pull away from the bone cleanly.

items.cause: Too much liquid, or the lid lifted repeatedly during cooking.
items.fix: If the sauce is too thin at the end, lift the shanks out and reduce the liquid in a saucepan over a high heat for 5 to 10 minutes.

items.symptom: Meat grey rather than rich brown
items.fix: The browning step is not optional. Pale meat in a slow cooker stays pale. Give the shanks real colour in a very hot pan before they go in.

content.text: Braised lamb shanks are a Sunday dinner or a winter weeknight meal that cooks while you are at work. Serve with mashed potato to absorb the sauce, or with soft polenta. The sauce is deeply savoury and rich from the dissolved collagen in the bone.
